notes: Attempted to reproduce 'Failed to bind EAGLDrawable' on iOS simulators (iOS 26.2 and iOS 18.2) with SkiaSharp 3.116.0 and .NET 10. SKGLView renders correctly on both simulators — blue background, red circle, and frame counter all display properly with no EAGL errors in logs. iOS simulators use the host macOS OpenGL stack for GL emulation rather than the actual iOS EAGL/GLKit hardware path, so the EAGLDrawable binding failure cannot manifest. A physical iOS device is required to reproduce this hardware-level GPU error. The underlying issue is real: SkiaSharp's iOS SKGLView uses deprecated OpenGL ES (EAGL) APIs that Apple deprecated in iOS 12, and the MAUI handler is already marked with [ObsoletedOSPlatform("ios12.0")].

versionResults.notes: SKGLView renders correctly on iOS 26.2 and iOS 18.2 simulators. Simulators use host macOS OpenGL, not real iOS EAGL hardware path.

reproductionSteps.description: Create MAUI project with SkiaSharp SKGLView targeting net10.0-ios
reproductionSteps.command: cd /tmp && dotnet new maui -n Repro && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.Views.Maui.Controls --version 3.116.0
reproductionSteps.output: The template "MAUI Application" was created successfully.

filesCreated.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:skia="clr-namespace:SkiaSharp.Views.Maui.Controls;assembly=SkiaSharp.Views.Maui.Controls"
             x:Class="Repro.MainPage">
    <Grid>
        <skia:SKGLView x:Name="GlView"
                       PaintSurface="OnGLPaintSurface"
                       WidthRequest="400"
                       HeightRequest="400" />
        <Label x:Name="StatusLabel"
               Text="Loading..."
               HorizontalOptions="Center"
               VerticalOptions="End"
               Margin="0,0,0,20"
               TextColor="White"
               FontSize="16" />
    </Grid>
</ContentPage>

filesCreated.description: GL paint handler drawing blue background, frame counter, and red circle

reproductionSteps.description: Build for iOS simulator (net10.0-ios, arm64 simulator)
reproductionSteps.command: cd /tmp/repro-3428/Repro && dotnet build -f net10.0-ios -r iossimulator-arm64 -c Debug
reproductionSteps.output: Build succeeded. 0 Error(s). 2 Warning(s) (obsolete API warnings for EAGL usage).

reproductionSteps.description: Install and launch on iPhone 17 Pro simulator (iOS 26.2)
reproductionSteps.command: xcrun simctl install 9D69AC7B iPhone17Pro Repro.app && xcrun simctl launch 9D69AC7B com.companyname.repro

reproductionSteps.description: Verify SKGLView rendering on iOS 26.2 simulator — check for EAGL errors and white canvas
reproductionSteps.command: xcrun simctl io 9D69AC7B screenshot screenshot-ios26.png && xcrun simctl spawn 9D69AC7B log show --predicate 'process == "Repro" AND (eventMessage CONTAINS "EAGL" OR eventMessage CONTAINS "framebuffer" OR eventMessage CONTAINS "fail")' --last 30s
reproductionSteps.output: Screenshot shows SKGLView rendering correctly: blue background, red circle, 'Frame: 2' text visible. No EAGL errors, no framebuffer errors, no white canvas. Only non-critical log entries: GSFontRegisterCGFont failed 305 (font), Socket error connecting to IDE (expected without debugger).

reproductionSteps.description: Cross-check on iOS 18.2 simulator to match reporter's likely iOS version
reproductionSteps.command: xcrun simctl create 'iPhone 15 Pro (iOS 18.2)' ... iOS-18-2 && xcrun simctl boot $UDID && xcrun simctl install $UDID Repro.app && xcrun simctl launch $UDID com.companyname.repro
reproductionSteps.output: Screenshot shows SKGLView rendering correctly on iOS 18.2: identical blue background, red circle, 'Frame: 2' text. No EAGL errors in logs. Only: libxpc assertion (non-critical), GSFontRegisterCGFont failed 305.

blockers: Requires physical iOS device — iOS simulators use host macOS OpenGL stack for GL emulation, not the actual iOS EAGL/GLKit hardware path. The 'Failed to bind EAGLDrawable' error is a hardware-level GPU binding failure that only manifests on real iOS devices where Apple has deprecated/restricted EAGL.

Reporter did not specify device model or whether issue was on simulator vs device (said 'Dunno' for devices). The EAGL error in comments came from user charlenni running 'MapPOC' app, suggesting a real device.

corrections.upstream: Triage classified as iOS platform issue reproducible in general
corrections.corrected: Bug is device-specific — iOS simulators use host macOS OpenGL and do not exhibit EAGL binding failures. Requires physical iOS device to reproduce.

actionability.reason: Could not reproduce on iOS simulators (iOS 18.2 and 26.2). SKGLView renders correctly on simulators because they use the host macOS OpenGL stack. Need confirmation this occurs on a physical device, and which device/iOS version specifically.

workarounds: Use SKCanvasView instead of SKGLView (CPU rendering, no EAGL dependency)
Use SKMetalView directly if Metal GPU rendering is needed (iOS already has Mac Catalyst Metal path in SkiaSharp)
missingInfo: Physical iOS device model where the error occurs
Exact iOS version on the device
Whether the issue occurs on simulator or only on physical device
Full log output showing the 'Failed to bind EAGLDrawable' error with context

proposedResponse.summary: Could not reproduce on iOS simulators; need device confirmation
proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for reporting this. We attempted to reproduce the "Failed to bind EAGLDrawable" error using a MAUI app with `SKGLView` and SkiaSharp 3.116.0 on .NET 10 (SDK 10.0.102).

**Result:** SKGLView renders correctly on iOS simulators (tested iOS 18.2 and iOS 26.2). No EAGL errors were observed.

This is expected because iOS simulators use the host macOS OpenGL stack for GL emulation, rather than the actual iOS EAGL/GLKit hardware path. The `EAGLDrawable` binding failure is a hardware-level GPU error that likely only manifests on physical iOS devices.

Could you help us narrow this down?
- **Device model:** Which physical iOS device shows this error?
- **iOS version:** What iOS version is running on the device?
- **Simulator vs device:** Does the issue occur only on a physical device, or also on the simulator?
- **Full log output:** The complete log showing the "Failed to bind EAGLDrawable" error with surrounding context

**Workarounds:**
- Use `SKCanvasView` instead of `SKGLView` (CPU rendering, no EAGL dependency)
- The underlying issue is that SkiaSharp's iOS `SKGLView` uses deprecated OpenGL ES (EAGL) APIs — Apple deprecated these in iOS 12. A migration to Metal for the iOS handler would be the proper fix.

**Tested:**
| Platform | iOS Version | Result |
|----------|-------------|--------|
| iPhone 17 Pro Simulator | iOS 26.2 | ✅ Renders correctly |
| iPhone 15 Pro Simulator | iOS 18.2 | ✅ Renders correctly |

actions.description: Add platform label for iOS and area label for views/rendering
